title: Virgin Galactic Stock Slides On Q1 Earnings, Company Targets Q4 Spaceflight
text: Virgin Galactic Holdings reported first-quarter results that beat expectations on revenue but disappointed investors, causing shares to decline in after-hours trading. The company generated $227,000 in quarterly revenue, surpassing analyst estimates of $190,000. This revenue came from access fees paid by future astronauts. However, Virgin Galactic posted a loss of 81 cents per share, though this beat projections for an 88-cent loss. The aerospace firm faced significant cash constraints during the period. The company consumed $54 million in operating cash flow and generated negative free cash flow of $93 million. Despite these challenges, Virgin Galactic ended the quarter with $251 million in total cash and equivalents. CEO Michael Colglazier stated: We've delivered the first of our new SpaceShips from our Assembly hangar to our Test-and-Launch hangar, ground testing of that SpaceShip is underway. The company remains optimistic about its timeline, planning to commence flight testing in the third quarter and commence spaceflight operations by year-end. Management projects second-quarter free cash flow between negative $87 million and $92 million but expects sequential improvement thereafter. Share price declined 1.04% in after-hours trading, settling at $2.85 per share following the earnings announcement.
